title: Extend byt new scaffolding for git repos
description: Add optional git-repo scaffolding support to byt new (clone repo or repo+subdir) so control plane can delegate scaffolding without duplicating logic.

title: byt memory should auto-create mmry store if repo detected but store doesn't exist
description: When byt detects a repo from cwd (via git remote or catalog), it should auto-create the mmry store if it doesn't exist, rather than falling back to govnr/default store.

title: Implement byt website sync command
description:
Add command to sync tool.toml files from workspace repos to website. Should:

1. Read website repo path from [website] config section
2. Walk workspace repos looking for tool.toml
3. Parse and validate against schema (govnr/examples/tool.toml)
4. Generate/update tool pages in website repo (config: toolz_dir)
5. Update toolz/index.js with tools array
6. Copy media files (icon, screenshot) to public/ (config: public_dir)
7. Be idempotent - only update if content changed
8. Support --commit flag to auto-commit changes
9. Support localized fields (tagline_de, description_de, etc.)

title: byt memory should auto-detect repo from cwd git remote, not just catalog
description: When running 'byt memory add' from ~/byteowlz/octo, it says 'At workspace root or outside workspace, using govnr store' even though octo is a valid git repo in the byteowlz workspace. byt should: 1) Check if cwd is inside a git repo, 2) Extract repo name from git remote or directory name, 3) Use that as the store name (creating if needed). Currently it only checks the catalog, which may not be up to date.

title: sort list for byt repo compare alphabetically by default
description: by default, the repo list when running byt repo compare should be sorted alpabetically with an optional flag for last changed dates

title: Remove agent-related commands now handled by agntz
bytdesign-system sync: vendored distribute-down command (mirror templates/schema)
description:
## Why

byteowlz/design-system needs to reach internal consumers (oqto now; future
screen-UI tools) WITHOUT going through npm publish. At N=1 consumer, npm is
all tax (login gating, semver ceremony, provenance) and zero benefit. The
source repo stays the single source of truth; consumers pull vendored
snapshots on demand.

This mirrors two patterns byt already owns:
- `schema` sync — moves files between a central repo and the catalog
- `templates` — distributes DOWN from byteowlz/templates into new projects

`design-system` is a distribute-down case (same shape as `templates`), so this
is NOT a new shape for byt — it generalizes an existing one.

## Command surface

```
byt design-system sync [--repo <name>]... [--ref <tag|sha>] [--build]
byt design-system status          # show drift vs pinned ref
byt design-system list            # show consuming repos + pinned refs
```

Default (no `--repo`): sync all repos with a `[design-system]` config block.

## Config block (per consumer repo) — `design-system.toml` or `[design-system]` in byt config

```toml
source = "byteowlz/design-system"   # central repo (github org/name)
ref    = "v0.1.0"                   # tag or sha, pinned & reproducible
impl   = "shadcn-ts"                # which impl dir to pull
path   = "frontend/vendor/design-system"  # where it lands in the consumer
```

Mirrors `SchemaConfig.repo_path` / `TemplatesConfig.repo` precedent.

## sync behavior

1. `git archive <ref> impls/<impl>/` from source repo (shallow, no history clone)
   — or shallow fetch + sparse-checkout if archive is unavailable.
2. Extract into `path`, overwriting.
3. Stamp `path/VENDORED.md` with: source url, ref, resolved sha, sync date,
   byt version. **This stamp is the load-bearing safety mechanism** — every
   vendored copy self-documents its origin, so drift is queryable, not silent.
4. `--build` flag (optional): run the consumer's build after sync (e.g.
   `bun run build`). Default off — let the consumer build in its own pipeline.

## status behavior

Compare each consumer's `VENDORED.md` sha against the source repo's latest tag.
Report: up-to-date / behind / diverged (local edits since sync).

## Two decisions baked in (see design-system ADR + oqto ADR-0014 supersession)

- **D1: sync src, not dist.** `dist/` is gitignored (build artifact) in the
  source repo. Syncing src keeps the source repo clean and mirrors how a real
  dep works; the consumer builds TS in its own pipeline. No coupling byt to
  the toolchain.
- **D2: whole package as local path/workspace dep, NOT flattened files.**
  Vendored dir keeps its package.json; consumer references it as
  `"file:./vendor/design-system"` or a workspace entry. Imports stay clean
  (`@byteowlz/design-system`) and it's a 1-line swap to a real npm dep if an
  external consumer appears later.

## Non-goals (explicit)

- This does NOT replace npm publish as a channel. The source repo can serve
  BOTH: byt for internal consumers, npm for external ones, same files.
- No build orchestration beyond the optional `--build` flag.
- No automatic drift detection cron — `status` is pull-based.

## Out of scope here

- The oqto cutover itself (oqto-trdk.9) — this command is the tool that makes
  it possible, but the cutover is tracked in oqto's trx.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `byt design-system sync` lands `impls/shadcn-ts/` src into a configured
      consumer path, stamped with origin/ref/sha/date.
- [ ] `status` reports drift vs latest source tag.
- [ ] Works against `byteowlz/design-system` ref `v0.1.0` (once tagged).
- [ ] Mirrors the config + handler + enum wiring style of `schema`/`templates`
      (no new architectural shape introduced).
- [ ] `--dry-run` honored (existing global flag).
- [ ] AGENTS.md command table updated.

## Refs

- Source repo: https://github.com/byteowlz/design-system
- Precedent commands: `schema` (main.rs:4474+), `templates` (TemplatesConfig ~main.rs:886)
- Consumer driver: oqto (oqto-trdk.9 base24 migration, blocked on this)
